---
title: "Writing"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
    title-block-banner: false
    title-block: false
---

::: {.landing-page .writing-landing-page}
::: {.essays-hero .writing-hero}
::: {.container .py-5}
::: {.row .align-items-end}
::: {.col-lg-8}

::: {.essays-kicker}
Writing Across Boundaries
:::

# Writing

::: {.essays-tagline}
Where research becomes conversation, and observation becomes language.
:::

:::

::: {.col-lg-4}

::: {.writing-hero-intro}
A curated collection of public essays, talks, reflections, and field-shaped writing—made to travel between science, leadership, and everyday life.
:::

:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.writing-featured .py-5 .py-lg-6}
::: {.container}
::: {.row .align-items-end .mb-4}
::: {.col-lg-8}

::: {.essays-section-label}
Featured Writing
:::

## A public reflection in five movements. { .h1 .mb-0 }

:::
:::

::: {.writing-featured-panel}
::: {.writing-featured-main}

::: {.writing-feature-meta}
Leadership · Reflection · Public Speaking
:::

### The Five Elements of Leadership

::: {.writing-feature-subtitle}
A graduation address for the California Department of Water Resources Emerging Leaders Cohort
:::

:::

::: {.writing-feature-side}

::: {.writing-feature-abstract}
Using the Five Elements—Metal, Water, Fire, Earth, and Wood—as a guiding metaphor, this essay reflects on leadership as something forged through pressure, sustained by trust, and measured by the conditions it creates for others to grow.
:::

[Read →](./five-elements-of-leadership.qmd){.essay-card-link}

:::
:::
:::
:::

::: {.writing-recent .py-5 .py-lg-6}
::: {.container}
::: {.row .align-items-end .mb-4}
::: {.col-lg-8}

::: {.essays-section-label}
Recent Writings
:::

## Public work, collected over time. { .h2 .mb-0 }

:::
:::

::: {.writing-recent-list}

::: {.writing-recent-item}
::: {.writing-recent-date}
Date forthcoming
:::

::: {.writing-recent-copy}
### [The Five Elements of Leadership](./five-elements-of-leadership.qmd)

A short public address on trust, steadiness, purpose, and growth in leadership.
:::
:::

:::
:::
:::

::: {.writing-topics-shell .py-5}
::: {.container}
::: {.row .align-items-end .mb-4}
::: {.col-lg-8}

::: {.essays-section-label}
Topics
:::

## Themes that keep returning. { .h3 .mb-0 }

:::
:::

::: {.writing-topics}
<span class="writing-topic">Leadership</span>
<span class="writing-topic">Ecology</span>
<span class="writing-topic">Data</span>
<span class="writing-topic">Landscape</span>
<span class="writing-topic">Ways of Thinking</span>
<span class="writing-topic">Public Speaking</span>
:::
:::
:::
:::
